pages.title: About Matt Vickers | Matt Vickers
pages.meta_description: Matt Vickers is Stockton born and bred. He loves our area and want to make life even better for local people.
headings: h2: Main navigation
h2: Meet Matt
h2: A history serving Stockton West
h3: Matt Vickers MP for Stockton South
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: About Matt Vickers | Matt Vickers Skip to main content Matt Vickers MP MP for Stockton West Main navigation Home About Matt Get in touch News In Parliament Getting Things Done Volunteer Matt Vickers MP MP for Stockton West About Matt About Matt Vickers Tweet Matt has a track record of standing up for Stockton ever since being proudly elected in 2019. Stockton born and bred and proud of it, Matt is driven by a genuine love of our area and will continue to work day and night to stand up for Stockton West. Born at North Tees, the son of a builder and a hairdresser, he has stacked shelves, pulled pints and laid bricks in Stockton West. Born locally, raised locally and educated locally, he knows the problems our area faces and is determined to solve them. Matt has a history in politics, having acted as constituency agent for titans of the political scene Matt knows how to get his point across. First working for William Hague and later running Former Prime Minister Rishi Sunak's successful election campaign, Matt was first elected as MP for Stockton South in 2019 and gave Teesside a known name in places of Government. Having acted as councillor for Hartburn Ward and led the opposition on Stockton-on-Tees Borough Council since May 2015, Matt has been working for the people of Stockton West for nearly a decade. Standing up for our Local high streets, Matt campaigned against the overdevelopment, crowding and congestion of towns and villages throughout Stockton and Yarm without the sustainable development to go with the growth. Having came from a background in retail and business, Matt has the interests of our highstreets and towns at heart and the solutions to go with it. Matt has taken every opportunity to visit constituents, local businesses and see the work of local residents and cannot wait to continue doing so as the MP for Stockton West. It's the best part of the job! Meet Matt If you want to know more about who Matt is, be sure to follow Matt on Facebook , Twitter and Instagram . A history serving Stockton West Conservative MP for Stockton West Proudly re-elected as MP for the new Stockton West constituency in July 2024. Appointed Shadow Minister of State for Crime, Police, and Fire. Conservative MP for Stockton South Proudly elected as MP for Stockton South in December 2019 Deputy Chairman of the Conservative Party since 2022 Standing to be MP for the new constituency of Stockton West in the 2024 General Election Chairman of the future of retail APPG. Chairman of Stockton Conservatives During his tenure as a councillor was the leader of the Conservative group on the council, campaigning against overdevelopment and for suitable parking facilities. Representing Hartburn Ward Represented Hartburn Ward on the Stockton-on-Tees Borough Council from May 2015 until April 2021. headings: h2: Main navigation
h3: I’m here to help you
h2: What is the role of a Member of Parliament?
h2: What an MP cannot do
h3: Matt Vickers MP for Stockton South
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: Get in touch | Matt Vickers Skip to main content Matt Vickers MP MP for Stockton West Main navigation Home About Matt Get in touch News In Parliament Getting Things Done Volunteer Matt Vickers MP MP for Stockton West Get in touch Tweet I’m here to help you I'm here to support you in any way I can and help to find a resolution to problems you may be facing with housing, welfare, immigration or a range of other issues. Please register below and I will investigate your case. We aim to respond to all queries within 30 days. You will always receive a written response. If you’ve already submitted a case request and haven’t yet heard back from me, we appreciate your patience in waiting for a reply. Please wait 30 days before contacting us again to chase progress. What is the role of a Member of Parliament? Members of Parliament (MPs) are elected to represent voters’ interests and concerns in the House of Commons and hold the government to account, as well as make laws. MPs consider laws (generally put forward by the Government) as well as raising issues that matter to voters in the House of Commons. This includes asking government ministers questions about current issues, including those that affect local people. MPs receives several hundred emails, letters, and phone calls every day. MPs often act as figureheads for local campaigns and help to raise awareness of important issues both locally and nationally. MPs do not have executive power, meaning they cannot give direct instructions to any government agency or public body, but they can contact them on behalf of constituents to raise concerns and ask questions. They also have no authority over local councils or councillors. MPs split their time between working in Parliament itself and working in the constituency that elected them. Please note, strict Parliamentary protocol dictates I can only deal with concerns relating to or respond to policy queries of constituents resident in Stockton West. If I am not your Member of Parliament, please re-direct your query to your own MP. If you are unsure, you can check this here . To request a surgery appointment or ask for Matt's assistance please email : [email protected] with the subject "SURGERY REQUEST" or via this link and the preliminary details of your case. What an MP cannot do An MP cannot intervene with legal matters or court cases, nor interfere with decisions previously made in court. They also cannot provide any financial or business advice. An MP is not able to assist in settling family arguments or private disputes with neighbours, employers or consumer matters. If your problem concerns the Local Government (Council), you should have exhausted the internal complaints procedure at the Council before you contact Matt. As an MP, Matt has no jurisdiction over planning applications or parking concerns. However, if you have exhausted the authority’s complaints procedure, Matt may write to the them, on your behalf, to highlight the concerns you have. If you are unsure of who to go to or you have a problem of a more general nature then your nearest Citizens' Advice Bureau will be able to guide you. Immigration: In certain cases, it is possible for your local Member of Parliament (MP) to assist with your immigration matter, there are strict rules which state what an MP can and can’t do and it is always important to remember that MP’s are not above the law and so cannot overturn decisions that have been made by the courts. MPs can do the following: Contact the Home Office to ask for the status of your application. If appropriate, raise cases with the Immigration Minister or Home Secretary directly. This could be if you have received a negative decision, have been waiting a very long time for a decision and want a response, if you are detained, or if you are facing removal or deportation. Chase delays - if there has been a long delay in a decision, your MP can push for a decision to be made. Make sure you want a decision, however, and be p

pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament | Matt Vickers Skip to main content Matt Vickers MP MP for Stockton West Main navigation Home About Matt Get in touch News In Parliament Getting Things Done Volunteer Matt Vickers MP MP for Stockton West In Parliament Tweet Here, you can see Matt's latest speeches and appearances in Parliament (provided by theyworkforyou): Rescue Services: Culture and Sports | Department of Health and Social Care | Written Answers To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the impact of the proposed amendments to the Health Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates I beg to move, That this House has considered funding for fire and rescue services. Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates Certainly there are the pressures of the summer and the consequences for rural communities. Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates The hon. Gentleman has the pleasure of representing a beautiful part of the world. Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates I could not agree more. More than ever, we are seeing fire services that are lean and efficient, having undergone all sorts of savings. Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates First, we have to see central Government funding in place to ensure that we can run safe and effective fire services. Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates I could not agree more. As the Fire Brigades Union puts it, these cuts are putting lives at risk. Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates A lot of fire services across the country are facing huge challenges. Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates It is hugely important that firefighters and communities are involved in those discussions, so that we can get the structure that works best Fire and Rescue Services: Funding | Westminster Hall debates I will make a little progress. The Government’s latest settlement takes things a step too far. pages.text_excerpt: Volunteer for Matt in your local area! | Matt Vickers Skip to main content Matt Vickers MP MP for Stockton West Main navigation Home About Matt Get in touch News In Parliament Getting Things Done Volunteer Matt Vickers MP MP for Stockton West Volunteer Volunteer for Matt in your local area! Tweet In only 5 years, Stockton has seen unprecedented levels of investment, progress and success - and we need to spread the word of our achievements! The Stockton West constituency covers the wards of Fairfield, Middleton St.George, Hurworth, Northern Parishes, Bishopsgarth, Elmtree, Thornaby (Village), Ingleby Barwick, Southern Villages, Hartburn, Yarm and Eaglescliffe. 👈 My team and I are always out delivering leaflets, attending campaign days and knocking on doors. Sign up using the form to the left if you can lend a hand! Thank you for your support so we can continue to transform our local area.
